questions.questionText: Which hook is used to manage state in a functional component?
questions.explanation: useState lets you add local state to a functional component.

questions.questionText: What does useEffect run after by default?
questions.explanation: By default, useEffect runs after every render unless a dependency array is provided.

questions.questionText: How do you pass data from a parent to a child component?
questions.explanation: Props are used to pass data from parent components to child components.

questions.questionText: Which array tells useEffect to run only once on mount?
questions.explanation: An empty dependency array [] means the effect runs only once, after the initial render.

questions.questionText: Which of these is a mutable data type in Python?
questions.explanation: Lists are mutable, meaning their contents can be changed after creation.

questions.questionText: What does the range(5) function generate?
questions.explanation: range(5) generates numbers from 0 up to (but not including) 5.

questions.questionText: Which keyword is used to define a function in Python?
questions.explanation: The "def" keyword is used to define a function in Python.

questions.questionText: What type of learning uses labeled data?
questions.explanation: Supervised learning algorithms are trained on labeled data.

questions.questionText: Which metric is commonly used for classification accuracy?
questions.explanation: F1 Score balances precision and recall and is widely used for classification tasks.

options: Performs well on training but poorly on new data
questions.explanation: Overfitting means the model has memorized the training data and fails to generalize.

questions.questionText: What is the time complexity of accessing an element in an array by index?
questions.explanation: Arrays provide constant time O(1) access via index.

questions.questionText: In a singly linked list, each node contains:
options: Data and a pointer to the previous node
Data and a pointer to the next node
questions.explanation: A singly linked list node stores data plus a reference (pointer) to the next node.

questions.questionText: Which operation is generally faster on a linked list than an array?
questions.explanation: Inserting at the beginning of a linked list is O(1), while arrays require shifting elements.
UX stands for User Experience, focusing on how a user feels using a product.

questions.questionText: Which principle suggests keeping similar elements visually consistent?
questions.explanation: Consistency helps users predict how interface elements behave across a product.

questions.questionText: A wireframe is best described as:
options: A low-fidelity layout blueprint of a screen
questions.explanation: Wireframes are simple, low-fidelity layouts used to plan structure before visual design.

options: A running instance of a container
A read-only template used to create containers
questions.explanation: A Docker image is a read-only template that contains instructions for creating a container.

questions.questionText: Which command is used to list running containers?
What file is commonly used to define a Docker image build process?
questions.explanation: A Dockerfile contains the step-by-step instructions to build a Docker image.
